channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Fijians predict when a cyclone is approaching using bees, breadfruit and ancient wisdom
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The Dutch police trained eagles to catch illegal drones, but the unusual experiment was later abandoned
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 10 largest lake islands in the world: From Manitoulin Island to Idjwi
item.description: Manitoulin Island in Canada is the world's largest freshwater lake island. René-Levasseur Island in Quebec was formed by a meteorite impact long ago. Sääminginsalo in Finland became an island after canal construction centuries ago. Olkhon Island in Russia's Lake Baikal features varied landscapes and cultural significance. These islands showcase diverse origins and remarkable freshwater environments globally.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US woman arrested for sexually assaulting 2-year-old; identified by 'boobs' tattoo
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Britain left a five-acre farm without crops; 15 years later, nature had turned it into...
item.description: An abandoned Norfolk farm field transformed into a vibrant wildflower meadow over a decade, as researchers monitored its evolution post-farming. Within ten to fifteen years, the area demonstrated an impressive resurgence of various plant species, emphasizing that simple, natural restoration techniques may outperform costly commercial methods. This evidence underscores the value of patience in reviving ecosystems and restoring lost habitats effectively.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian truck driver Jashanpreet Singh jailed for nearly 5 years for deadly California crash
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why was Washington reflecting pool drained again weeks after $14M renovation
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump admin sued by 9 environmental groups over definition of 'harm'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Promise Me, America': Joe Biden to release new memoir on November 17 after US midterm polls
item.description: Joe Biden will release his new memoir, 'Promise Me, America,' on November 17. The book reflects on his presidency and his decisions regarding re-election. It will cover major events like the pandemic and international conflicts. The memoir's release follows the upcoming US midterm elections. Biden also mentioned his ongoing cancer treatment and recovery progress.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists found that white streetlights can trap more than 5,000 pill bugs in giant circular “death spirals,” exposing them to predators and wasting their energy
item.description: Researchers discovered land isopods forming massive, synchronized circular formations. These creatures, known as woodlice, were drawn into glowing traps by streetlights. The isopods marched in tight circles until exhaustion or predation stopped them. This behavior, observed in Israel's Golan Heights, poses risks to the small animals. Artificial light at night can inadvertently alter natural survival strategies.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Bali’s 1,000-year-old rice fields once worked as a giant water system. Now 6,500 hectares have vanished and locals are buying water
item.description: Bali's historic subak irrigation system is under pressure from tourism expansion. Thousands of hectares of rice fields have disappeared, impacting water availability. Increased demand from tourism facilities strains groundwater resources significantly. This loss threatens the subak system and ecological balance on the island. Preserving these terraces is crucial for water security and cultural heritage.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Singapore turned wastewater into drinking water: How Singapore has treated the sewage water into liquid gold through NEWater, achieving a sustainable supply that exceeds World Health Organisation standards
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'I intend to kill Muslims': Indian mall worker in US stabbed by Utah man
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists cut climbing vines from a damaged Borneo forest. Its canopy then grew three times faster
item.description: Trimming aggressive climbing vines from logged tropical forests can substantially expedite canopy recovery, providing a more economical alternative to new tree planting for ecosystem restoration. Areas where vines were cut exhibited a carbon storage rate three times quicker than those with newly planted trees, as confirmed by laser mapping. Effective vine management promotes forestry sustainability and preserves biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: For nearly 50 years, China has placed straw grids across its deserts; desertified land is now shrinking by over 1,000 sq km a year, but scientists say the fight is far from over
item.description: China's Green Great Wall project has fought desertification for nearly fifty years. Millions of rural laborers have used straw checkerboards and planted saplings. This effort has reduced desertified land and increased forest cover significantly. The program demonstrates a long-term political commitment to environmental restoration. Sustaining these gains requires continued local involvement and adaptation.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Iran says 30 civilians killed in US strikes as Trump warns of 'hitting them harder'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Kick Argentina out': Why over 10 million people signed petition against Messi's team
item.description: An online petition demands Argentina's expulsion from the World Cup due to alleged FIFA favouritism. Millions have signed the petition, citing perceived bias towards Lionel Messi and his team. Critics point to controversial refereeing decisions and VAR reviews in several matches. Argentina's coach dismisses these claims, calling them baseless accusations from detractors. The winners of the Argentina-England semi-final will face Spain in the championship match.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This little girl has one adorable morning routine with her three Golden Retriever pet dogs
item.description: A little girl begins her day with morning cuddles for her three Golden Retrievers. She hugs Riley, Twyla, and Bear individually before any other activity. The dogs remain calm and affectionate during these daily morning greetings. This routine teaches the child about love and gentle connection. The heartwarming video captures a special bond between a child and her pets.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: British man jailed in Iran on spying charges given additional two-year sentence for speaking to media, family claims
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists warmed the same forest soil by 5°C for 37 years; in the fourth decade, microbes began releasing carbon once thought safely locked away
item.description: Forest soils are releasing more carbon than previously believed. Warming temperatures cause stable soil organic matter to decompose. This decomposition releases additional carbon dioxide into the atmosphere. The process creates a stronger climate feedback loop for global warming. Updated climate models will now account for this slow carbon release.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
